Deploying the Uniface Server Docker Image

Deploying a Uniface Server Docker image entails pulling the image, creating the Uniface Application context on the Docker host with a volume mount or environment variables, and deploying and running the Uniface Server application.

To deploy a Uniface Docker image:
  1. Pull the Uniface Server image using the following Docker command: 
    docker pull rocketsoftware/uniface:latest

    The :latest tag will pull the most recent patch level, and is the default if not specified. If you require an earlier version, substitute the tag as appropriate. All available tags can be found here: Docker Hub registry.

  2. Create the Uniface Application context by copying the compiled objects and configuration to a local folder on your Docker host. The container expects this to be at /home/uniface/application and mounts this folder at runtime.
    Note: Your host folder must have an adm subfolder containing the Uniface configuration (.asn and .ini files) as well as all relevant Uniface application resources (UAR files).

    Within the container, the USYS Path Logical USYSADM points to /home/uniface/application/ adm and USYSLOG to /var/log/uniface. At start-up, the container does the following:

    1. Executes the initialization script /home/uniface/unifacerc
    2. Changes the current directory to the Uniface working directory /home/uniface/project
    3. Starts the Uniface Router. For the Uniface Router to start successfully, /home/uniface/application/adm must be present and contain usys.asn, urouter.asn, and any other relevant Uniface configuration files.

    An example file structure could look like this, however you can adapt this for your specific requirements:

    /home/uniface/application
├── adm
│ ├── dbms.asn
│ ├── urouter.asn
│ ├── userver.asn
│ ├── usys.asn
│ ├── usys.ini
│ └── wasv.asn
└── uars
 ├── patch.uar 
 └── application.uar

    As part of creating the application context, you must also specify a valid license configuration, for example:

    ; usys.asn specifying a license file
[SETTINGS]
$license_options = LM_LICENSE_FILE=/home/uniface/application/lservrc

    or:

    ; usys.asn specifying a license server
[SETTINGS]
$license_options = LM_LICENSE_FILE=Sentinel_RMS_license_server_IP_or_DNS_name

    If you are using a Cloud LM license:

    ; usys.asn specifying no license file or license server
[SETTINGS]
$license_options = LM_LOAD=S

    You can also use the LSHOST environment variable, which will be discussed later. Use LSHOST to enter your Registration Token if you have a Cloud LM license.

  3. Use the command line docker run to run a Docker container. For example: 
    docker run --rm -p 13005:13001 -v /app/payroll:/home/uniface/application/ -it rocketsoftware/uniface:latest
    In this example, -pexposes the internal port of the Uniface Router, and -vvolume mounts your local application context to the container's application context. For a full range of Docker arguments, see Docker run reference.

    In addition to using docker run, you can also use Docker Compose, which uses a YAML-based configuration file to deploy one or more containers.

    Environment Variables

    The Uniface Server Docker image supports some configuration through the use of environment variables. These are set using the switch -e with docker run. The following environment variables can be set:

    • AUTH_PASSWORD—Password to connect to the Uniface Server, overriding the default password.
    • REGTOKEN—The registration token for your Cloud LM license. You need the USRVCTR license feature to do this.
    • LSHOST—IP or DNS name of your Sentinel RMS license server. You need the USRVCTR license feature to do this.

    It is good practice to never use default passwords, especially in production.

    Patching

    Docker images and containers should be treated as immutable, meaning that they should not be patched in place as you might do with standard deployments. To update your image, pull a new Uniface image.

    The published Uniface Server docker image always includes the latest patch. You do not need to download and apply patches to the image yourself. To update to the latest image:

    1. Stop and remove the running container(s).
    2. Pull the required image using the :latest tag, or the tag for the specific patch level you require.
    3. Run your container.

    As your Uniface solution components are loaded from a volume mount, you can add the patch UAR to the host volume and restart the container. However, it is better practice to treat the containers as immutable and create new versions of them.

    Logging

    Logging is disabled in the Docker image by default. To enable logging you have the following options:

    • Standard out—logs get written to stdout in the container. You can view them with the command docker logs. You need to set $putmess_logfile /dev/pts/0 in your assignment file.
    • Volume mount—logs get written to /var/log/uniface. To do so, volume mount this directory to make the logs persistent, and set $putmess_logfile usyslog:userver%p.log in your assignment file. 
      docker run -v <local log dir>:/var/log/uniface --rm --it rocketsoftware/uniface
    • Docker syslog—logs get written to stdout in the container, Docker forwards them via syslog. You need to set $putmess_logfile /dev/pts/0 in your assignment file. 
      docker run -log-driver syslog --log-opt syslog-address=udp://<ip>:<port> --rm --it rocketsoftware/uniface

    For all options, set $ioprint in your assignment files.
